query: 1. What are the common symptoms of diabetes?
chunk: Diabetes mellitus is a chronic metabolic condition in which the body is unable to effectively regulate blood glucose levels. This may occur due to insufficient insulin production or resistance to insulin action. Over time, elevated glucose levels can have widespread effects on various organ systems. The early symptoms of diabetes often develop gradually and may go unnoticed. Individuals commonly experience increased thirst and frequent urination as the body attempts to eliminate excess glucose through urine.

A fasting blood glucose level of 126 mg/dL
